rationale: Both correctly identify PR #4792 as the fix for the non-async-chunk branch shipping (None, payload) instead of the full payload, matching the ground truth's diff and confirming it's already folded into the 0.24 rebase (a560ed18) — core diagnosis is equally correct. X is more thoroughly grounded, tracing the full failure path through the scheduler coordinator's WAITING_FOR_INPUT/_FULL_PAYLOAD_INPUT_STAGES logic and timeout messages that plausibly match the reporter's log, and even proactively dismisses red herrings from the issue body. Y pads its report with a 'dedicated regression test' (test_sample_tokens_tail_only_prefix_cache_uses_staged_cpu_hidden_states) whose name has nothing to do with inter-stage payload routing, which looks like a fabricated/misapplied verification claim and slightly undercuts its grounding and completeness despite an otherwise solid, well-structured answer.
